title: Elaphrium capense
taxonomy: Elaphrium capense is classified as a genus of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, also known as legumes. It is native to the southwestern part of the continent of Africa. Further classification places Elaphrium capense within the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The plant is often found growing in sandy soils in arid regions. Within the family Fabaceae, Elaphrium capense is a member of the tribe Phaseoleae.

Euphorbia is a succulent plant genus that comprises approximately 2,000 species, characterized by its unique and diverse shapes, sizes, and growth habits. The health benefits of Euphorbia capsaica, also known as the candelabra tree, include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Euphorbia capsaica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Euphorbia is often used to make various preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
